message:
fix(grpc): HealthWatcher returns ServingStatus directly, not Option

HealthWatcher::status() and watched_status() previously returned
Option<ServingStatus>, requiring callers to handle None for unregistered
services. The gRPC health Watch contract defines unregistered services
as ServiceUnknown, so wrapping in Option added unnecessary ambiguity.

Changes:
- watched_status() returns ServingStatus, mapping None to ServiceUnknown
  for both named and empty-string (server-wide) service lookups
- HealthWatcher::last_status field changed from Option<ServingStatus> to
  ServingStatus
- status() returns ServingStatus directly
- poll_status() returns (bool, ServingStatus) with single-read
  semantics: reads current status once, compares against last, updates
  last, returns both the change flag and current status atomically
- Update all test assertions across grpc_production_features and
  web_grpc_reference_services to use direct ServingStatus comparisons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
